key_items: Bitcoin preps 3% May downside, but US PMI data may boost BTC price
Bitcoin's 114-Day Sideways Drift Set to End With 20% Move This Week - high volatility breakout imminent
New US inflation report leaves Bitcoin with a problem - PCE inflation at 3.8% year-on-year, hottest in 2 years
reasoning: Mixed signals dominate: bearish technical weakness (May down 3.5%, consolidating near support at $72,500) and hot inflation data (3.8% PCE) conflict with bullish institutional catalysts (Michael Saylor buy signals, CFTC perpetual futures approval, $1B Iran seizure supporting potential Trump Bitcoin reserve). Bitcoin sits at a critical inflection point with historical volatility compression suggesting an imminent 10-20% directional move but direction remains uncertain.
risks: Breakdown below $72,500 support combined with sustained high inflation forcing Fed hawkishness could trigger a sharp 10-15% correction despite positive regulatory developments.
